Global (All).title: Taliban bans girls from attending school above grade six
Global (All).summary: The Taliban has banned girls from attending school above the sixth grade, sparking widespread outrage and protests across Afghanistan. The move is seen as a major setback for the country's education sector and a blow to girls' education rights.

Global (All).title: Russia-Ukraine Conflict Escalates
Global (All).summary: The conflict between Russia and Ukraine has escalated in recent days, with reports of heavy shelling and fighting in several regions. The situation remains tense and volatile, with concerns about a wider conflict.

Global (All).title: Protests Erupt in Iran Following Government Crackdown
Global (All).summary: Protests have erupted in Iran following a government crackdown on dissent. The protests are seen as a challenge to the government's authority and a threat to regional stability.

United States.title: Supreme Court Rules on Student Loan Forgiveness: Potential Implications for Economic Inequality
United States.summary: A recent US Supreme Court ruling has sparked concerns over potential impacts on economic inequality and social mobility in the country.

United States.title: Increased Tensions Between the US and China Over Taiwan Relations
United States.summary: Escalating tensions between the US and China over Taiwan's future have raised concerns about the potential for increased conflict and economic instability.

United States.title: Growing Protests in the US Over Racial Injustice and Police Brutality
United States.summary: Widespread protests across the US have highlighted ongoing concerns about systemic racism and police brutality, with some demonstrations turning violent.

South Korea.title: North Korea threatens US and South Korea with 'fiercest' military action over new sanctions
South Korea.summary: North Korea has threatened the US and South Korea with 'fiercest' military action over new sanctions imposed by the US. The country's state-run news agency KCNA said the sanctions are 'a reckless provocation' that will lead to 'the most fierce and merciless punishment'.

South Korea.title: South Korean government introduces new regulations to prevent cryptocurrency hacking
South Korea.summary: The South Korean government has introduced new regulations to prevent cryptocurrency hacking, including a ban on cryptocurrency exchanges from offering new services and a requirement for exchanges to implement enhanced security measures.

South Korea.title: Protests erupt in South Korea over government's handling of COVID-19 pandemic
South Korea.summary: Protests have erupted in South Korea over the government's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic, with thousands of people taking to the streets to demand greater transparency and accountability.

Japan.title: Japan's LDP leader Kishida set for easy election win: poll
Japan.summary: Japan's ruling Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) leader Fumio Kishida is on track to win the party presidential election, a survey showed yesterday. The poll by the Nikkei newspaper found 87.1% of LDP supporters intended to vote for Kishida, while 5.4% backed former Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi, the only other candidate.

Japan.title: Japan's economy shrinks for first time since 2021, hurt by weak consumption and exports
Japan.summary: Japan's economy shrank at an annualized rate of 1.0% in the first quarter, its first contraction since 2021, driven by a slump in consumer spending and exports. The contraction was larger than the median market forecast of a 0.8% contraction.

Japan.title: US-Japan relations strained as Japan re-examines military cooperation
Japan.summary: Tensions between the US and Japan have increased in recent days as Tokyo reviews its military cooperation with Washington. Japan's new defense strategy, set to be unveiled in the coming months, is expected to limit the country's military support for the US in a possible conflict with China.
